name: Loop quantum gravity program
summary: Classification: TOE-ASPIRATIONAL (incomplete matter sector). Background-independent canonical quantisation of GR using Ashtekar connection variables and a loop / spin-network basis, augmented by loop quantum cosmology for the early-universe regime. Predicts discrete area and volume spectra at the Planck scale and a non-singular quantum bounce replacing the Big Bang. Distinguished from string-theoretic TOE candidates by being background-independent from the start and by not requiring extra dimensions or supersymmetry. Listed here as a TOE-aspirational program with the explicit acknowledgement that LQG has no canonical matter sector: the framework as it stands closes the quantum-gravity-consistency puzzle but leaves the entire SM sector externally specified. Real LQG papers either treat matter as a Fock space on the spin-network kinematics (Thiemann) or remain agnostic; the catalog reflects this by limiting composes_mechanisms to LQG itself.

lineage.citation: Ashtekar, 'New variables for classical and quantum gravity', PRL 57, 2244 (1986)
Rovelli and Smolin, 'Spin networks and quantum gravity', PRD 52, 5743 (1995)
Bojowald, 'Absence of singularity in loop quantum cosmology', PRL 86, 5227 (2001)
Ashtekar, Pawlowski, Singh, 'Quantum nature of the big bang', PRL 96, 141301 (2006)
Rovelli and Vidotto, 'Covariant Loop Quantum Gravity', Cambridge (2014)
